**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   The image represents a process flow diagram rather than a quantitative graph, so I cannot extract quantitative data.

**3. Tables:**
*   None

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Title:** Fig.1. The key role of ammonia as an energy carrier in future energy systems [7].
*   **Key Components:**
    *   Production
        *   Fossil fuels: Natural gas -> Steam Reforming (Natural Gas & Steam -> Syngas. CCS-CO2 sequestration)
        *   Renewable Energy: Solar, Wind, Hydro -> Water Electrolysis (H2O -> O2, H2)
        *   Air Distillation (Air, liquidifed -> N2, O2) -> Ammonia Synthesis Plant
        *   Ammonia Synthesis Plant (N2, H2 -> Comp.-> RX -> NH3)

    *   Storage & Transport:
        *   Ammonia Storage
        *   Ammonia Transportation (NH3 tank)
        *   NH3 Pipeline

    *   Utilization:
        * CO2-free Hydrogen Production  (NH3 decomposition & H2 purification)
        *   H2-Fuel Cell Vehicles
        *   H2-Power Generation (Turbine, SOFC)
        *   Ammonia direct use Power generation (NH3-fueled SOFC, Reciprocal Engine -> Electrical furnace, Gas turbine)
        *   Residential/Industrial (Transportation)

*   **Legend:**
    *   Fossil fuels
    *   Renewable energy
    *   Hydrogen
    *   Nitrogen
    *   Carbon dioxide
    *   Ammonia
    *   Electricity

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   11% reduction in energy demand via efficiency improvements
*   Sevenfold increase in renewables from 19% (2019) to 79% by 2050
*   Ammonia energy density: ~3 kWh/L

*   NOx emissions reduced by 25% when using cracked ammonia (80% cracked) compared to uncracked ammonia-hydrogen mixture.
*   Thermal efficiency can be enhanced by as much as 3.5% when combining ammonia-powered gas turbines and concentrated solar power facilities.

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   T_e ≈ T_i ≈ T < 1 eV
*   T_e ≈ T_i ≈ 0.03 eV
*   T_i << T_e < 10 eV
*   T_e ≈ T_i > 100 eV

**2. Graphs/charts:**

*   **Figure 30: Plasma Classification**
    *   This is a hierarchical diagram outlining different types of plasmas
        *   Plasma
            *   Thermal equilibrium plasma
                *   T_e ≈ T_i ≈ T < 1 eV Arc discharge
            *   Nonequilibrium plasma
                *   T_e ≈ T_i ≈ 0.03 eV; T_i << T_e < 10 eV Corona discharge, Plasma jet, Dielectric Barrier discharge, Low pressure glow discharge
            *   High temperature plasma
                *   T_e ≈ T_i > 100 eV Fusion Plasma, Solar Core

*   **Figure 31: Principle of plasma assisted combustion**
    *   Diagram components and labels:
        *   Plasma discharge
            *   O_2, N_2: Ions/electrons
            *   NO, O, O, H, OH: Radicals int. species
            *   N_2* ,n_2(v), O_2(a^1 Delta_g): Excited species
            *   H_2, CO, CH_4, CH_2O: Fuel fragments
            *   Ionic wind instability
        *   Temperature increase
        *   Thermal
        *   Kinetic
        *   Transport
        *   Combustion Enhancement

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_TABLE_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:** See descriptions under "Graphs/charts"

*   Airflow compression process raises temperatures to 700 °C.
*   Thermal/Environmental Barrier Coatings (T/EBCs) can withstand temperatures exceeding 1500 °C.
*   Technology Readiness Level (TRL): Scales from 1 to 9.
**Units:**

*   eV (electron volts)
*   °C (degrees Celsius)

*   Units:
    *   Year
    *   Temperature: °C
*   Curves:
    *  "Conventionally cast" (Ni-based superalloys): Starts at ~750°C in 1965, increasing to ~900°C in 2025
    *   "Directionally solidified": Starts at ~800°C in 1965, increasing to ~1000°C in 2025
    *   "Single-crystal": Starts at ~950°C in 1965, increasing to ~1150°C in 2025
    *   "With TBCs" (Thermal Barrier Coatings): Starts at ~1000°C in 1965, increasing to ~1400°C in 2025
    *   "With TBC, film cooling" (Thermal Barrier Coatings, film cooling): Starts at ~1000°C in 1965, increasing to ~1500°C in 2025
    *   "With T/EBC, cooling" (Environmental Barrier Coatings, cooling): Starts at ~1250°C in 1965, increasing to ~1600°C in 2025
*   Key values: The graph shows an approximate trend of maximum allowable gas temperatures with different cooling technologies.

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

*   Hybrid system (solar energy and fuel cells): Discharge efficiency of 49%, overall energy efficiency of 55.7%.
*   Hybrid SOFC cycle: Enhanced energy and exergy efficiencies by 27.8% and 26.6%
*   Hybrid SOFC, single generation (internal reforming): Over 60% efficiency, exceeded 80% efficiency with waste heat recovery
*   Hybrid Solid Oxide Fuel Cell SOFC -steam turbine 1.5MW: 60% electrical efficiency
*   Organic Rankine Cycle cogeneration based on SOFC: 67% overall efficiency
*   SOFC: 68% thermal efficiency
*   SOFC gas-turbine tri gen: 43.2 % efficiency
